summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a legendary ’69 show highlighted by a soaring 30‑minute Dark Star and flawless Eleven/Death‑Don’t performances.
review: The recording is an excellent early‑'69 soundboard tape – reviewers repeatedly call the mix “crisp”, “flawless” and “the best sound quality” of that era. Musically the show is a showcase for the band’s peak psychedelic improvisation, with a monumental Dark Star (≈30 min), a fire‑charged Eleven, a soulful Death‑Don’t‑Have‑No‑Mercy and strong St. Stephen/Cold Rain & Snow selections. The few mixed comments are outweighed by overwhelming enthusiasm.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, the vast majority are 5‑star and extol both performance and audio fidelity; show quality (70 %) and recording quality (30 %) both rate at the top of their scales.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with superb instrument separation; multiple reviewers call it “flawless”, “best sound quality” and note the tape’s high‑fidelity capture of early ’69 ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Partial show but the highlighted sequence (Dark Star → Feelin' Groovy jam → Tighten Up jam → St. Stephen → The Eleven → Death‑Don’t) flows seamlessly; reviewers repeatedly praise the segues and the high‑octane energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his soaring guitar tone, lyrical improvisations on Dark Star and a soulful vocal delivery on Death‑Don’t.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “popping” bass lines, especially during The Eleven where his rhythmic drive shines.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom (Hammond) receives specific praise; reviewers mention his organ work adding depth to the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming described as crystal‑clear and a highlight of The Eleven; several reviews call the percussion “remarkable”.
